Detailed Description

Provides an object to get access to image data and its properties.

The Image object is used to retrieve, store and convert images obtained from a camera. Image provides a set of methodes to get information about, and work with an image (such as pixelformat, size and offset).

When retrieving an image from a camera, the Image object will reference an image-buffer without copying it's data for performance reasons. If you copy the Image object using the "=" operator the image data will still not be copied but referenced. If you want to copy image data please use the Image::Copy() or Image::Convert() methods

Examples
edgedetect_cuda.cpp, edgedetect_opencl.cpp, getting_started.cpp, opencv.cpp, opencv_cap.cpp, and user_buffer.cpp.

Member Function Documentation

◆ operator=()

const Image& NeoAPI::Image::operator= ( const Image &  object)

The "=" operator of the Image object.

Note
The "=" operator will not copy the actual image data but reference it. To create an actual copy of the image data please use the Image::Copy() or Image::Convert() methods
Parameters
[in]objectAn Image object
Returns
The Image object

Check if the image is empty or filled with data.

False if the image is filled with data, otherwise true

virtual bool NeoAPI::Image::IsLastImage ( ) const
virtual

gives information if this image is the last image in a buffer

Note
calling this on a common single image buffer returns true
Returns
true if image is the last image in a buffer, otherwise false

list of available PixelFormats

virtual bool NeoAPI::Image::IsPixelFormatAvailable ( const NeoString &  pixelformat) const
virtual

Indicates whether the PixelFormat is available for conversion.

Parameters
[in]pixelformatThe target PixelFormat name
Returns
True if the pixelformat is available to work with, otherwise false
Examples
opencv.cpp.

◆ GetImageIndex()

virtual uint64_t NeoAPI::Image::GetImageIndex ( ) const
virtual

Get the Index of the image inside the buffer, returns zero as long the image is not filled with data.

Only when the image belongs to a GenDC buffer this value will be greater than one.

Returns
The Index of the image
See also
CamBase::GetImage()

◆ GetBufferID()

virtual uint64_t NeoAPI::Image::GetBufferID ( ) const
virtual

Get the ID of the buffer, returns zero as long the image is not filled with data.

The buffer ID allows to track which buffers have been received and if there is a gap which might show that an buffer was missed and not processed.

Returns
The ID of the buffer holding the image
See also
CamBase::GetImage()

◆ GetTimestamp()

virtual uint64_t NeoAPI::Image::GetTimestamp ( ) const
virtual

Get the timestamp of the image, returns zero as long the image is not filled with data.

The image timestamp allows to track when images have been recorded and if there is a gap which might show that an image was missed and not transfered.

Returns
The timestamp of the image
See also
CamBase::GetImage()

◆ GetImageData()

virtual void* NeoAPI::Image::GetImageData ( ) const
virtual

Get a pointer to the image data, returns nullptr as long as the image is not filled with data.

Returns
The pointer to image data
See also
CamBase::GetImage()
Examples
opencv.cpp, and opencv_cap.cpp.

◆ GetChunkList()

virtual FeatureList& NeoAPI::Image::GetChunkList ( ) const
virtual

Get a list of all available chunk features of the image.

Returns
A list of available chunk features
See also
FeatureList

◆ Copy()

virtual Image NeoAPI::Image::Copy ( ) const
virtual

Copies the Image object and its image data.

Note
Copy works only with uncompressed images.
Memory related meta data like SegmentSize will be copied also.
Returns
A new Image object
Exceptions
NoImageBufferExceptionThe image could not be copied.
See also
CamBase::GetImage()

◆ Save()

virtual void NeoAPI::Image::Save ( const NeoString &  filename) const
virtual

Save the image in bmp format at the given path.

If no path is given, the image will be stored where the executable is started

Parameters
[in]filenameThe filename or path for the saved image
Exceptions
FileAccessExceptionThe image could not be saved at the given Path
See also
CamBase::GetImage()
Examples
getting_started.cpp, and user_buffer.cpp.

◆ Convert() [1/2]

virtual Image NeoAPI::Image::Convert ( const NeoString &  pixelformat) const
virtual

Convert the Image to another PixelFormat.

A camera can stream images in different formats. However this might not be the format you need to work with the image data. You can use this method to convert an image from one format to another. (e.g. convert BayerRG8 to BGR to work with the image data using OpenCV).

Please see NeoAPI::PixelFormat for a list of possible formats

Note
Not all input formats can be converted to all output formats. You can use the Image::GetAvailablePixelFormats() or the Image::IsPixelFormatAvailable() to check if a conversion is possible
Parameters
[in]pixelformatThe target PixelFormat name
Returns
A new Image in requested PixelFormat
See also
Image::GetAvailablePixelFormats()
Image::IsPixelFormatAvailable()
Examples
opencv.cpp.

◆ Convert() [2/2]

virtual Image NeoAPI::Image::Convert ( const ConverterSettings &  settings) const
virtual

Convert the Image based on the provided settings.

A camera can stream images in different formats. However this might not be the format you need to work with the image data. You can use this method to convert an image from one format to another. (e.g. convert BayerRG8 to BGR to work with the image data using OpenCV).

Additionally you may want to apply sharpening or want to change the color temperature. This can be achieved by providing the corresponding settings.

Please see NeoAPI::PixelFormat for a list of possible formats

Note
Not all input formats can be converted to all output formats. You can use the Image::GetAvailablePixelFormats() or the Image::PixelFormatIsAvailable() to check if a conversion is possible
Parameters
[in]settingsThe settings for the converted image.
Returns
A new Image with requested Settings

◆ GetUserBuffer()

template<typename T >
T NeoAPI::Image::GetUserBuffer ( ) const
inline

Get access to a object inherited by NeoAPI::BufferBase and used by this image.

Attention
When the image object goes out of scope the underlying NeoAPI::BufferBase object will be reused for image acquisition again. This means the content of registered memory region may change then.
Returns
UserBuffer object
Examples
edgedetect_cuda.cpp, edgedetect_opencl.cpp, and user_buffer.cpp.

◆ _GetUserBuffer()

virtual BufferBase* NeoAPI::Image::_GetUserBuffer ( ) const
protectedvirtual

Get access to the NeoAPI::BufferBase used by this image.

This function should not used directly. Use Image.GetUserBuffer() instead.

See also
Image.GetUserBuffer()
Returns
NeoAPI::BufferBase object

virtual uint64_t NeoAPI::ImageInfo::GetXPadding ( ) const

Return the the number of extra bytes transmitted at the end of each line.

The x padding of the image in byte

◆ GetPixelFormat()

virtual NeoString NeoAPI::ImageInfo::GetPixelFormat ( ) const
virtualinherited

Get the pixelformat of the image, returns an empty string as long the image is not filled with data.

The GenICam SFNC defines many different PixelFormats which are used to transfer image data from a camera such as RGB, YUV and BayerRG. Knowledge about it is essential so the image can be converted to a format needed to process it

Note
More information about the GenICam SFNC (Standard Feature Naming Convention) can be found in the GenICam SFNC Document on the EMVA Website
Returns
The PixelFormat of the Image

◆ GetSize()

virtual uint64_t NeoAPI::ImageInfo::GetSize ( ) const
virtualinherited

Get the whole size of the image in bytes.

Returns
the image size in bytes

Note
in case of a non GenDC buffer this returns always 0

the group id
